ontologyAdditions.name: Euler Method
ontologyAdditions.definition: Approximating a first-order initial-value problem with the explicit Euler update yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + h f(xₙ, yₙ), using one slope evaluation per step.
reviewNotes: The active ontology had only an Improved Euler node whose exclusions explicitly reject ordinary Euler.
A distinct leaf is required to represent AQA 7367 J2 without a false concept link.

ontologyAdditions.name: Mode of a Discrete Random Variable
ontologyAdditions.definition: A value of a discrete random variable having the greatest probability mass; more than one mode may occur.
exclusions: sample-data mode without a probability model
reviewNotes: The official statement explicitly assesses the mode of a discrete random variable.

ontologyAdditions.name: Median of a Discrete Random Variable
ontologyAdditions.definition: A value locating the 0.5 probability quantile of a discrete random variable, determined from cumulative probability.
exclusions: sample-data median without a probability model
reviewNotes: The official statement explicitly assesses the median of a discrete random variable.

ontologyAdditions.name: Exponential Distribution Moments
ontologyAdditions.definition: For an exponential distribution with rate λ, the mean is 1/λ and the variance is 1/λ², with standard deviation 1/λ.
exclusions: generic continuous moments without the exponential model
reviewNotes: The active exponential-distribution branch lacked a leaf for its explicit moments.

removals.reason: Function-graph transformations are unrelated to counting combinations.
The statement concerns generic function modelling, not specifically exponential models.
Small-angle approximations are not the Pythagorean trigonometric identity.
The statement assesses differentiation of powers, not integration of exponentials.
The statement assesses differentiation of exponentials and trigonometric functions, not integration.
The statement assesses integration of powers, not integration of exponentials.
Contextual numerical methods are not a ratio-and-proportion concept.
The official statement explicitly excludes reduction formulae.
The statement assesses Venn diagrams for conditional probability events, not Venn diagrams as an abstract set-theory topic.
The statement assesses general vector addition and scalar multiplication, not force-vector notation.
The statement explicitly excludes calculation of the mean and variance of discrete random variables.
The statement assesses a hypothesis test for a population mean, not calculation of a descriptive mean.
The statement concerns derived quantities and their SI units rather than rectilinear acceleration as a motion concept.
The statement assesses projectile kinematics under gravity, not Newton-law dynamics in a vertical line.
The statement assesses multiplication and division in modulus-argument form, not conversion between Cartesian and polar forms.
The official statement assesses matrix representations of linear transformations, not elementary geometric construction transformations.
The statement assesses closed-form sums of powers, not the classification of integers.
The statement concerns transformations of function curves, not geometric reflection constructions.
The statement concerns transformations of function curves, not geometric translation constructions.
The statement concerns the integral average value of a function, not a descriptive-statistics arithmetic mean.
The statement concerns the calculus arc-length functional for general curves, not the length of a circular arc.
The statement concerns vector distances among lines, points and planes in three dimensions, not only the elementary planar point-to-line construction.
The official statement specifies ordinary Euler's step-by-step method; the current node explicitly excludes ordinary Euler and represents improved Euler.
The statement assesses vector kinematics in circular motion, not scalar acceleration in rectilinear motion.
The acceleration formulae are radial acceleration in circular motion, not general rectilinear acceleration.
The statement assesses moments and location measures of a discrete random variable, not a descriptive-data standard deviation node.
In context, “this distribution” refers to the discrete uniform distribution in the immediately preceding statement, not the Normal distribution.
The statement assesses the parameter-derived moments of the Poisson distribution, not generic descriptive standard deviation.
The statement assesses the Poisson identity E(X)=Var(X)=λ, not generic descriptive mean.
The statement assesses moments derived from a continuous density, not generic descriptive standard deviation.
The statement assesses proofs of the continuous-uniform moments, not generic descriptive standard deviation.
A chi-squared association statistic is unrelated to a two-sample t procedure.
Interpreting cells that drive a chi-squared association is not interpretation of a correlation coefficient.
The official statement specifies a one-sample t test for a population mean, not a two-sample t procedure.
The statement assesses a confidence interval for a population mean with known variance, not descriptive mean.
The statement assesses a large-sample confidence interval for a population mean, not descriptive mean.
The statement assesses a t confidence interval for a population mean, not descriptive mean.
The statement assesses distribution-specific exponential moments, not generic descriptive standard deviation.
A resource histogram in project scheduling is not a statistical frequency histogram.
The official statement names distance-time graphs, while this node explicitly represents displacement-time graphs.
The official statement names frequency trees, while this node explicitly excludes frequency trees and represents probability trees.

methodology: All AQA statements remained local and were not sent to Kimi, DeepSeek, OpenAI, or another external model.
The local review compared atomic statementText, notesText, and examplesText against comparison-eligible ontology leaves.
Only corrections with an exact source substring and an explicit ontology scope match are proposed.
